title: Python SCHC cannot transmit non-CoAP UDP (raises SchcError in production path); Rust/C compress it as CoAP
description: Python whole-packet profiles match any UDP packet >= 4 payload bytes (headers.py:192-206, no CoAP version check), then codec.compress enforces CoAP.version EQUAL 1 (codec.py:143-144) and RAISES SchcError. compress_packet (headers.py:491-497) has no exception fallback and the production caller is unprotected (python/src/lichen/node.py:484). Rust (codec.rs:269-323) and C (schc.c:736-806) extract type/tkl/code/MID from whatever bytes are present and emit rule 0/1 - round-trips fine (fields are pure residue passthrough), so C<->Rust interop works even for non-CoAP UDP. Consequence: Python cannot originate MQTT-SN or any non-CoAP UDP application at all; a Python node crashes on such traffic while Rust/C nodes forward it. Fix: add SchcError->rule-255 fallback in Python compress_packet (and guard node.py), or implement MQTT-SN rules in Python.
